content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of NNN non-negative integers, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) in the following way:


Consider all partitions of the array AAA into continuous subarrays, A[L1,R1],A[L2,R2],…,A[LK,RK]A[L_1, R_1], A[L_2, R_2], \ldots, A[L_K, R_K]A[L1​,R1​],A[L2​,R2​],…,A[LK​,RK​] (you can choose KKK), where

1≤Li≤Ri≤N1 \le L_i \le R_i \le N1≤Li​≤Ri​≤N
L1=1L_1 = 1L1​=1, RK=NR_K = NRK​=N
Ri=Li−1+1R_i = L_{i - 1} + 1Ri​=Li−1​+1


The score of a partition is defined as ∑i=1K(ALi∣ALi+1∣ALi+2∣…∣ARi)\sum_{i = 1}^{K} (A_{L_i} | A_{L_i + 1} | A_{L_i + 2} | \ldots | A_{R_i})∑i=1K​(ALi​​∣ALi​+1​∣ALi​+2​∣…∣ARi​​), where ∣|∣ represents the Bitwise OR operator.
Less formally, the score is the sum of Bitwise ORs of all subarrays.
A partition is called optimal if and only if it has the minimum possible score over all partitions.
f(A)f(A)f(A) is defined as the maximum possible KKK that can be used in an optimal partition.
You are given an array AAA of NNN non-negative integers.

Find ∑L=1N∑R=LNf([AL,AL+1,AL+2,…,AR]\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} f([A_L, A_{L + 1}, A_{L + 2}, \ldots, A_R]∑L=1N​∑R=LN​f([AL​,AL+1​,AL+2​,…,AR​], i.e. the sum of fff over all subarrays of AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the value of ∑L=1N∑R=LNf([AL,AL+1,AL+2,…,AR]\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} f([A_L, A_{L + 1}, A_{L + 2}, \ldots, A_R]∑L=1N​∑R=LN​f([AL​,AL+1​,AL+2​,…,AR​].
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 333 subarrays:

[1][1][1] : Only one possible division, which is trivially optimal. It has K=1K = 1K=1.
[2][2][2] : Only one possible division, which is trivially optimal. It has K=1K = 1K=1.
[1,2][1, 2][1,2] : There are 222 possible divisions, one is [1,2][1, 2][1,2] and the other is [1]+[2][1] + [2][1]+[2]. Both are optimal, because both have a score of 333. Thus, the maximum KKK is 222.

Hence, the sum is 1+1+2=41 + 1 + 2 = 41+1+2=4.
